notes.title: meteor1
notes.content:
# STUDY NOTES: ATMOSPHERIC RADIATION & EARTH'S ENERGY BUDGET
## I. ELECTROMAGNETIC MECHANICS
 * Radiation Mechanics: Energy transferred via electromagnetic (EM) waves through space or a material medium. It requires no physical medium to propagate.
 * Wave Architecture: Oscillating electric and magnetic fields traveling mutually perpendicular to each other and to the direction of propagation.
 * Dual Nature: Radiation operates simultaneously as continuous transverse waves and discrete particle packets (photons).
 * Spectrum Breakdown: Ultraviolet spans 0.01–0.4 µm, visible light spans 0.4–0.7 µm, and thermal infrared spans 0.7–100 µm.
 * Inverse Energy Rules: Frequency (f), wavelength (\lambda), and energy (E) are governed by c = \lambda f and E = hf. Shorter wavelengths carry higher energy.
 * Solar vs. Terrestrial: The Sun emits high-energy shortwave radiation peaking at ~0.5 µm (visible light). Earth emits lower-energy longwave radiation peaking at ~10 µm (thermal infrared).
## II. FUNDAMENTAL RADIATION LAWS
 * Blackbody Concept: A hypothetical perfect absorber and emitter that radiates energy based strictly on its temperature. The Sun (~6000 K) and Earth (~288 K) approximate blackbodies.
 * Planck’s Law: Governs spectral radiance as a definitive function of wavelength and temperature.
 * Wien’s Displacement Law: Dictates peak emission wavelength. Higher temperatures yield shorter peak wavelengths. Formula: \lambda_{\text{max}} = \frac{2897}{T}.
 * Stefan-Boltzmann Law: Total emitted energy scales rapidly to the fourth power of absolute temperature. Formula: E = \sigma T^4.
 * Kirchhoff’s Law: High-efficiency absorbers at a specific wavelength act as high-efficiency emitters at that exact same wavelength. Example: CO₂ absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation.
## III. ATMOSPHERIC RADIATIVE PROCESSES
 * Extinction vs. Emission: Atmospheric constituents alter radiation fields. Extinction (scattering + absorption) attenuates radiative intensity, while emission increases it.
 * Absorption Mechanics: Removes radiative energy and converts it to internal heat. Absorptivity (\alpha) dictates the exact fraction absorbed.
 * Selective Atmospheric Absorbers: O₃ selectively absorbs shortwave UV. H₂O and CO₂ selectively absorb longwave infrared.
 * Emission Mechanics: All physical bodies above absolute zero (0 K) emit radiation. Real surfaces operate below blackbody efficiency, measured by emissivity (\varepsilon < 1).
 * Scattering Mechanics: Redirection of radiation by atmospheric particles without converting the wave energy into heat.
| Scattering Type | Particle Size vs. Wavelength | Wavelength Dependence | Observable Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rayleigh | Very small | Strong (targets short waves) | Blue skies, red sunsets |
| Mie | Comparable | Weak | Haze, whitish pollution glare |
| Non-selective | Large | None (equal across spectrum) | White clouds |
## IV. INSOLATION & ALBEDO
 * Solar Constant: Incoming solar radiation arriving at the top of the atmosphere averages ~1361 W/m².
 * Solar Composition: Incoming insolation arrives as 7% UV, 44% visible light, and 49% near-infrared.
 * Insolation Modulators: Surface intensity is dictated by the Solar Zenith Angle (lower sun angle = lower intensity per unit area), latitude, cloud cover, and seasonal axial tilt.
 * Albedo Mechanics: The exact fraction of solar radiation reflected back by a surface. Measured on a scale from 0 (total absorption) to 1.0 (perfect reflection).
 * Surface Albedo Baselines: Fresh snow reflects 80–90%, open ocean reflects 5–10%, and Earth's average planetary albedo sits at ~30%.

notes.title: meteor1
notes.content:
* Albedo Variables: Driven by soil composition, soil moisture, vegetation cover, and urbanization density.
## V. PLANETARY ENERGY BUDGET (100% IN = 100% OUT)
 * Total Incoming Solar: 100% entering the top of the atmosphere.
 * Planetary Albedo (30% Reflected): The Earth reflects 30% of total insolation back to space (20% off clouds, 6% off the atmosphere, 4% off the surface).
 * Planetary Absorption (70% Captured): The Earth absorbs 70% of incoming energy (51% into land/oceans, 16% into the atmosphere, 3% into clouds).
 * Surface Dissipation (Latent Heat): 23% of absorbed surface energy is carried upward into the atmosphere stored within evaporated water vapor.
 * Surface Dissipation (Atmospheric Capture): 15% is radiated upward as thermal infrared and trapped by atmospheric gases.
 * Surface Dissipation (Thermals): 7% is transferred upward via direct surface conduction and convection (rising air).
 * Surface Dissipation (Direct Space): 6% is radiated directly from the ground through atmospheric windows straight to space.
 * Total Outgoing Equilibrium: 70% of longwave radiation returns to space (64% emitted by the atmosphere/clouds + 6% emitted directly by the surface), perfectly balancing the 70% absorbed.
## VI. CLIMATE DYNAMICS & OBSERVATION
 * Greenhouse Mechanism: Atmospheric gases (CO₂, H₂O, CH₄) capture outgoing terrestrial infrared and re-radiate it downward, sustaining Earth's habitable baseline temperature.
 * Anthropogenic Forcing: Human-driven accumulation of greenhouse gases forces a net planetary energy imbalance.
 * Feedback Loops: Water vapor acts as a positive feedback (warming generates more vapor, trapping more heat). Clouds act as a negative feedback (warming generates more cloud cover, reflecting more incoming sunlight).
 * Field Instrumentation: Pyranometers measure incoming shortwave solar radiation. Pyrgeometers measure outgoing longwave infrared.
 * Satellite Tracking: Platforms like MODIS and CERES capture real-time planetary radiation budgets.
